sidebar.title: MCLang API
intro.title: Welcome to MCLang API
intro.desc: A modern Web API interface designed for MCLang. Here you can fetch all Minecraft translation files and dictionaries, and automate text generation.
card1.title: 1. Setup Connection
card1.desc: Go to 'Auth & Connection' on the left to verify your API server address. If the server requires security authentication, ask your admin for an API Key and fill it in.
card2.title: 2. Check Supported APIs
card2.desc: The API provides Language List Query and Translation Parsing. It supports multiple versions of official Minecraft translation language packs.
card3.title: 3. Run Tests
card3.desc: Enter parameters in the 'API Testing' area below to dynamically get responses without writing code, allowing you to preview formats and content.
auth.title: Auth & Connection
auth.desc: Define the base URL of the backend API. If the requireKey security mechanism is configured, set the key here.
auth.url.label: API Server Address (Base URL):
auth.key.label: Enter API Key (Bearer Token):
test.title: API Testing (Playground)
test.desc: Select an endpoint below and input parameters to interact. Unauthorized requests will be blocked by the server.
ep1.desc: Get a list of all supported languages and versions on the current server.
ep2.desc: Get a translation list for a specific language.
ep3.desc: Get a single translation string for a specific language.
console.title: Server Response
console.default: // Click 'Execute' to see the API response...
alert.unauth: Access Denied (401 Unauthorized). Please check if your API Key is correct.
api.title: Developer Java API Guide
api.desc: How to use MCLang as a dependency in your Bukkit plugin and fetch localized translations via the API.
api.maven.title: 1. Import Dependency
api.maven.desc: Add MCLang-API to your project's pom.xml or build.gradle.
api.code.title: 2. Code Example
api.code.desc: Use the MCLang class to instantiate different languages and retrieve localized translation strings for items, entities, or enchantments.
